Top centered title: **Cognitive Neuroscience: The Biology of the Mind**

Centered subtitle below: **Integration of (at least) four disciplines:**

Four discipline lines, with discipline names in red and explanations in black:

- **Neurology:** Function and pathology of the nervous system
- **Neuroscience:** Mechanisms of the nervous system  
  Includes  
  neuroanatomy  
  neurophysiology  
  neurochemistry
- **Cognitive psychology:** How the mind processes information
- **Computer science/Artificial intelligence:**  
  How can computers perform tasks  
  Can we simulate behavior from neural-like models.

Layout details: title at top; subtitle centered beneath; four left-aligned entries stacked vertically; “Includes” under “Neuroscience:” with three indented subfields listed on separate lines; under “Computer science/Artificial intelligence:” two indented question lines on separate lines.

Title: **Antecedents in Behavioral Neurology**

Text:
- **Emphasis on linking brain parts to different tasks**
  - **e.g.,** language and the left hemisphere
  - coordination and the basal ganglia

Subheading:
- **Diagram makers**

Diagram: lateral view cross-section of the brain, labeled **FRONT** at left top and **BACK** at right top.

Brain labels:
- **Anterior Cingulate** *(motivation)*
- **MOTOR**
- **SENSORY**
- **FRONTAL LOBE** *(planning)*
- **PARIETAL LOBE** *(movement)*
- **OCCIPITAL LOBE** *(vision)*
- **TEMPORAL LOBE** *(hearing)*
- **Dorsolateral Prefrontal** *(executive & logical)*
- **Olfactory Bulb**
- **Lateral Orbitofrontal Prefrontal** *(socially & emotional regulatory)*
- **Hypothalamus**
- **Amygdala** *(emotion)*
- **Entorhinal Cortex** *(memory)*
- **Hippocampus** *(memory)*
- **CEREBELLUM** *(movement coordination)*
- **BRAIN STEM** *(body basics)*
- **LIMBIC SYSTEM**
- **CORTEX**

Colored regions distinguish lobes and internal structures; arrows/leader lines connect each label to its anatomical location.

Title centered at top: “Antecedents in Behavioral Neurology”

Bullet-style text below, left aligned:

- “Emphasis on linking brain parts to different tasks”
- “Important for clinical evaluation and treatment”

Indented examples beneath:
- “Predicting recovery from language problems”
  “after a stroke.”
- “Prescribing medication for elderly person who”
  “shows onset of coordination problems.”

Title centered at top: “Antecedents in Behavioral Neurology”

Bullet-style text below, left-aligned:
- “Emphasis on linking brain parts to different tasks”
- “Important for clinical evaluation and treatment”
- “Task-based analysis is limited for explaining “how””

Indented question text below:
- “What do we learn about language from knowledge
  that left hemisphere is critical for speech
  comprehension and production?”

Further question text below:
- “Or understand basal ganglia role in movement from
  discovery that this structure is site of pathology in
  Parkinson’s disease?”

Cognitive Psychology

Provide detailed analysis of how information is processed when we perform a task

Suggests reading involves two types of representations  
Phonology: Conversion of visual representations to sounds (e.g., syllables)  
Orthography: (Skilled) readers directly process the entire word (the irregular words)

Interdisciplinary effort to relate mental processes to brain structures.

This relationship works in both directions
